name: Giant Hogweed
description: A massive herbaceous plant dangerous to humans due to phototoxic sap that causes severe burns.
ecosystemImpact: Displaces native species, forms monocultures, reduces biodiversity.
humanDanger: Sap causes severe burns on contact with skin and sunlight.
spreadWays: Self-seeding, seed dispersal by water and wind, transport with soil.
identificationTips: Large dissected leaves, hollow stem with purple spots, enormous umbels of white flowers.
controlDescription: Must be destroyed before flowering. Full protective clothing is required when handling.

name: Box Elder Maple
description: A fast-growing deciduous tree that aggressively colonizes new territories.
ecosystemImpact: Displaces native tree species, alters soil composition.
humanDanger: Pollen causes allergies. Brittle branches are dangerous in strong winds.
identificationTips: Compound leaves of 3-5 leaflets, greenish flowers in clusters, winged seeds at acute angles.
controlDescription: Effective clear-cutting followed by stump treatment.

name: Canadian Goldenrod
description: A perennial herbaceous plant with yellow panicle-shaped flower clusters.
ecosystemImpact: Forms dense thickets, suppresses other plants, releases allelopathic chemicals.
identificationTips: Small yellow flowers in pyramidal panicles, lanceolate leaves with serrated edges.
controlDescription: Mow before flowering 2-3 times per season.

name: Wild Cucumber
description: An annual vine with clinging tendrils and prickly fruits.
ecosystemImpact: Wraps around trees and shrubs, suppressing their growth.
humanDanger: Not dangerous to humans, but may cause allergies.
spreadWays: Seeds dispersed by water and animals.
identificationTips: Lobed leaves, small white flowers, green prickly gourd-like fruits.
controlDescription: Easily pulled by hand before fruiting.

name: Black Locust
description: A deciduous tree with delicate foliage and fragrant white flowers.
ecosystemImpact: Alters soil composition by enriching it with nitrogen, harming native plants.
humanDanger: Bark, leaves, and seeds are toxic. Thorns can cause injuries.
identificationTips: Compound odd-pinnate leaves, paired thorns on shoots, white fragrant flowers in clusters.
controlDescription: Requires complete removal of the root system.

name: Himalayan Balsam
description: An annual plant with succulent stems and pink flowers.
ecosystemImpact: Displaces riparian vegetation, alters soil structure.
humanDanger: Seed pods explode on contact, scattering seeds.
spreadWays: Seeds dispersed by water and animals.
identificationTips: Succulent stems, lanceolate leaves, pink or purple flowers in clusters.
controlDescription: Easily pulled by hand before fruiting.

name: Garden Lupine
description: A perennial plant with palmate leaves and vivid flower spikes.
ecosystemImpact: Alters soil composition by enriching it with nitrogen.
humanDanger: Seeds are toxic to humans and animals.
spreadWays: Seeds dispersed by wind and animals.
identificationTips: Palmate leaves, tall spikes of blue, purple, or pink flowers.

name: Sakhalin Knotweed
description: A large herbaceous plant with enormous leaves.
ecosystemImpact: Forms monocultures, suppressing all other plants.
identificationTips: Huge oval leaves, hollow stems, small white flowers.

name: American Pokeweed
description: A large herbaceous plant with dark purple berries.
humanDanger: All parts of the plant are toxic.
identificationTips: Large oval leaves, white flowers in clusters, dark purple berries.
controlDescription: Complete removal of the root system is required.

name: Red Oak
description: A large deciduous tree with leaves that turn red in autumn.
identificationTips: Deeply lobed leaves with bristle tips, leaves turn red in autumn.

name: Tatarian Honeysuckle
description: A shrub with pink flowers and red berries.
ecosystemImpact: Actively spreads in forests and forest edges.
identificationTips: Oval leaves, pink flowers, fused red berries.

name: False Indigo Bush
description: A shrub with compound leaves and dark purple flowers.
identificationTips: Compound leaves, dark purple flowers in clusters, pods.

name: Silver Buffaloberry
description: A shrub with silvery leaves and small flowers.
identificationTips: Silvery scaly leaves, small yellow flowers, silvery fruits.
controlDescription: Cutting with removal of root suckers.

name: Late Cherry
description: A deciduous tree with dark bark and white flowers.
humanDanger: Fruits are edible and used in jams and wines, but pits contain cyanogenic compounds and should not be consumed.
identificationTips: Dark bark, glossy leaves, white flowers in clusters, black fruits.

name: Cut-leaf Coneflower
description: A tall herbaceous plant with yellow flowers.
ecosystemImpact: Forms thickets along roads and in wastelands.
identificationTips: Tall stem, dissected leaves, yellow flowers with green centers.

name: Goat's Rue
description: A perennial legume with blue-violet flowers.
ecosystemImpact: Forms dense thickets, alters soil composition.
humanDanger: Toxic to animals when consumed in large quantities.
identificationTips: Compound leaves, clusters of blue-violet flowers, pods.

name: Wood Small-reed
description: A perennial grass forming dense tussocks.
identificationTips: Long narrow leaves, flower panicles, forms dense patches.

name: New England Aster
description: A perennial plant with purple flowers.
identificationTips: Tall stem, lanceolate leaves, purple flowers with yellow centers.
